decks.notes: The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures and pump them up with cheap spells to apply pressure on the opponent early in the game. The deck emphasizes efficient card draw and manipulation to ensure a consistent flow of threats and burn spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing a mix of aggression and card advantage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize and mount a defense.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays using a combination of creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, Scion of Draco, and Territorial Kavu, alongside powerful support cards such as Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding to control the board. Quantum Riddler adds a disruptive element to the deck, while Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger provides additional pressure. Stubborn Denial offers protection against removal spells, ensuring the deck can maintain its aggressive momentum. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the various color requirements of the spells in the deck.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor, then use Primeval Titan to search for lands like The Mycosynth Gardens and Boseiju, Who Endures to protect your combo pieces. The ultimate goal is to use cards like Scapeshift and Cultivator Colossus to put multiple powerful lands into play and overwhelm your opponent quickly. The deck combines control elements with a powerful combo finish to create a versatile and competitive strategy in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to consistently apply pressure with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, while using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to clear a path to victory. The deck also includes disruption elements like Mana Tithe and Ranger-Captain of Eos to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively through a combination of creature damage and burn spells.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo involving Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate massive amounts of value and overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Malevolent Rumble, the deck can control the board and set up its combo pieces. Emrakul, the Promised End and Kozilek's Command provide additional threats and disruption. The inclusion of Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga allows for quick ramp and consistency in finding key pieces. Overall, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and win through its powerful synergy and combo potential.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards that allow for disrupting opponent's strategies and searching for key combo pieces to quickly generate mana and win the game through a powerful finisher like Goblin Charbelcher. It aims to control the board through counterspells and removal while efficiently assembling its win condition. The deck doesn't rely on traditional land cards for mana production, allowing for a unique and streamlined strategy that can catch opponents off guard. It emphasizes careful resource management and strategic decision making to outplay opponents and secure victory.

The deck focuses on playing cheap artifacts and using them to power up synergies, creating a fast-paced aggression. It makes use of cards like Kappa Cannoneer, Mox Opal, and Mishra's Bauble to generate mana and card advantage, while also utilizing removal spells like Metallic Rebuke and Tormod's Crypt to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Urza's Saga is a key card that can generate additional threats and provide extra value. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures, disruptive spells, and powerful artifacts.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quick, aggressive plays with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Slickshot Show-Off, backed up with burn spells like Lava Dart and Lightning Bolt. It also includes card advantage and selection through cards like Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain momentum throughout the game, aiming to close out the match before the opponent has time to stabilize. It also includes some flexibility with cards like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge to disrupt opponent's strategies and protect its own creatures.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays, utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly put pressure on the opponent. It also includes utility cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer for added value. The deck also features a mix of removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board. Additionally, it includes some land disruption with Blood Moon and Static Prison to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, it aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creature plays and disruption.

This deck aims to manipulate the graveyard by cycling creatures with low mana costs, then casting Living End to return all those creatures to the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Street Wraith and Endurance to cycle through your deck quickly while disrupting the opponent's strategy. Generous Ent and Shardless Agent help to cascade into Living End, while Force of Negation protects your combo. The deck also includes some powerful creatures like Colossal Skyturtle and Waker of Waves to provide additional threats.

The deck focuses on assembling the Broodscale Bloodchief combo by playing creatures like Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker while utilizing cards like Blade of the Bloodchief and Kozilek's Command to generate value and disrupt opponents. Emrakul, the Promised End provides a powerful finisher, with support from cards like Malevolent Rumble. The deck also includes support cards like Urza's Saga and Ancient Stirrings to help find key pieces of the combo. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful and synergistic creature-based combo strategy.

The key concept of this Aggro-Hollow One deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards like Faithless Looting and Burning Inquiry to enable powerful plays with cards like Hollow One, Ox of Agonas, and Detective's Phoenix. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Lightning Bolt and Cool but Rude to control the board while pushing for damage. Dragon's Rage Channeler and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide additional threats and card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast creatures and burn spells to close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of mana. It utilizes powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Thought-Knot Seer, along with support cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms, to disrupt the opponent's strategy. With access to a variety of removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent until it can close out the game with its powerful threats.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of efficient removal spells, hand disruption, and counterspells. It also aims to control the board with powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck utilizes a mix of traditional control elements with unique cards like Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog to create a versatile and adaptable strategy. The land base is designed to provide consistent mana sources while also offering flexibility in fetching key cards. Ultimately, the deck aims to outlast and outsmart opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

The deck revolves around the strategy of quickly reanimating powerful creatures or spells from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending. The combo potential is further enhanced with instant-speed reanimation and control elements like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize. Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Solitude provide strong threats on the battlefield while Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to surprise the opponent with fast, powerful plays and overwhelming them with a mix of reanimation, control, and aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and produce a large amount of mana quickly. This enables the player to cast Primeval Titan and search for powerful lands like Urza's Saga and Simic Growth Chamber. By ramping up mana, the player can combo off and either take multiple extra turns with Scapeshift or overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures from the Primeval Titan searches. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and overwhelm the opponent with powerful land and creature interactions.
